title: Pyrus favargeri
intro: Pyrus communis var. favargeri, commonly known as Pyrus faurgeri, is a variety of the pear tree species (Pyrus communis). The health benefits of Pyrus faurgeri include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to promote cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus faurgeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Pyrus faurgeri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
taxonomy: Pyrus favargeri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Rosaceae. This species belongs to the genus Pyrus, which encompasses various pear tree species.
